start.speaker: Master Aldric
start.text: Hmm. A new student, wandering into my library without so much as a knock. Most first-years don't find their way here until their second month. You must be curious — that's good. My name is Aldric. I teach Arcane Theory.

choices.label: Sorry to intrude. I was just looking around.

aldric_eager.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_eager.text: Eager. Good. But eagerness without discipline is dangerous. Come to my lectures with an open mind and a closed mouth, and you may yet learn something. Now — is there something specific you need?

choices.label: Can you tell me about Arcane Theory?
I'll come back when I have a question.

aldric_polite.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_polite.text: At least you have the decency to admit it. The library is open to all students. Just be careful with the ancient texts — some of them bite. Metaphorically. Mostly.

aldric_biting_books.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_biting_books.text: The Grimoire of Unbound Shadows is... temperamental. Don't touch the red shelf. *he returns to his reading*

aldric_arcane_theory.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_arcane_theory.text: Arcane Theory is the foundation of all magic. Every spell, every card, every enchantment draws on the same fundamental force — mana. Understanding how mana flows and accumulates is the key to mastering any element. Most students ignore this and wonder why their spells fizzle.

aldric_returning.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_returning.text: Ah, [Player]. Back again. I was beginning to wonder if you'd gotten lost. What brings you to the library today?

choices.label: I wanted to ask about the lost tome.

aldric_lost_tome.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_lost_tome.text: The tome... yes. I've been trying not to dwell on it. 'Foundations of Elemental Convergence' — irreplaceable. I misplaced it several days ago and it's been gnawing at me. If you see it in your travels, I would be most grateful for its return. Try asking around — perhaps that peculiar merchant at the market has seen it.

aldric_guidance.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_guidance.text: Guidance. *sighs* Very well. My advice: spend less time socialising and more time in here. The Academy's libraries hold wisdom that most graduates never tap into. Every answer you need is in a book — you just have to know which one to read.

aldric_tome_return.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_tome_return.text: Have you spoken with Zephyr about the tome yet? I suspect they may know something — they seem to know everything that passes through the market.

choices.label: I have the tome right here.
Not yet. I'll go ask them.

aldric_tome_returned.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_tome_returned.text: My tome! *he clutches it with visible relief* I... thank you, [Player]. Truly. I know I can seem cold, but this book represents decades of my research. Your help means more than you know.

aldric_tome_done.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_tome_done.text: Ah, [Player]. Your assistance with the tome was... I don't say this often, but it was exemplary. I'm adjusting my assessment of you upward.

aldric_lessons_greeting.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_lessons_greeting.text: Ah, [Player]. I've been reviewing your progress. I think you're ready for some advanced theory. Shall we begin?

aldric_lesson_done.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_lesson_done.text: Excellent. I've prepared a scroll containing one of my more... accessible advanced spells. Consider it homework. *he hands you the Arcane Scroll*

aldric_secret_greeting.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_secret_greeting.text: [Player]... I need to speak with you about something that doesn't leave this library. The vault beneath us — the sealed one — it contains records the headmaster doesn't want students to find. I've been here long enough to know something is wrong.

aldric_vault_records.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_vault_records.text: Expulsion records. Disciplinary actions. Students who were silenced. I believe Aria's sister wasn't the only one. There may be dozens of cases the Academy buried. I need someone I can trust — someone without an official stake — to help me investigate.

aldric_final.speaker: Master Aldric
aldric_final.text: [Player]. I've spent thirty years at this Academy, and in all that time, I have never met a student who reminded me so much of who I was before the politics ground me down. Don't let this place take that from you.

choices.label: I won't. Thank you, Master Aldric.
